checks.query:
-- table_stats.sql
-- Size metrics and the planner's estimated row count for a single relation.
-- Read-only. Placeholders are substituted with quoted string literals
-- (schema name, table name, and qualified name passed to the pg_*_size calls).
SELECT
    pg_total_relation_size('public.customer')                 AS total_bytes,
    pg_table_size('public.customer')                          AS table_bytes,
    pg_indexes_size('public.customer')                        AS index_bytes,
    pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size('public.customer')) AS total_pretty,
    pg_size_pretty(pg_table_size('public.customer'))          AS table_pretty,
    pg_size_pretty(pg_indexes_size('public.customer'))        AS index_pretty,
    c.reltuples::bigint                                        AS estimated_rows
FROM pg_catalog.pg_class c
JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
WHERE n.nspname = 'public'
  AND c.relname = 'customer'

checks.message: Row count estimate requires online mode
checks.query:
-- table_stats.sql
-- Size metrics and the planner's estimated row count for a single relation.
-- Read-only. Placeholders are substituted with quoted string literals
-- (schema name, table name, and qualified name passed to the pg_*_size calls).
SELECT
    pg_total_relation_size('public.customer')                 AS total_bytes,
    pg_table_size('public.customer')                          AS table_bytes,
    pg_indexes_size('public.customer')                        AS index_bytes,
    pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size('public.customer')) AS total_pretty,
    pg_size_pretty(pg_table_size('public.customer'))          AS table_pretty,
    pg_size_pretty(pg_indexes_size('public.customer'))        AS index_pretty,
    c.reltuples::bigint                                        AS estimated_rows
FROM pg_catalog.pg_class c
JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
WHERE n.nspname = 'public'
  AND c.relname = 'customer'

SELECT
    min(created_at) AS earliest,
    max(created_at) AS latest
FROM public.customer

SELECT count(*) AS duplicate_groups, coalesce(sum(c) - count(*), 0) AS extra_rows
FROM (
    SELECT id, count(*) AS c
    FROM public.customer
    WHERE id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY id
    HAVING count(*) > 1
) d

SELECT count(*) AS duplicate_groups, coalesce(sum(c) - count(*), 0) AS extra_rows
FROM (
    SELECT email, count(*) AS c
    FROM public.customer
    WHERE email IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY email
    HAVING count(*) > 1
) d

SELECT count(*) AS orphans
FROM public.customer c
WHERE c.org_id IS NOT NULL
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.org p WHERE p.id = c.org_id)

-- column_stats.sql
-- Per-column null and cardinality profile. Read-only.
-- The column placeholder is a quoted identifier; the relation placeholder is a
-- FROM-clause expression (optionally a LIMIT sample subquery).
SELECT
    count(*)                                  AS total_rows,
    count(*) FILTER (WHERE id IS NULL)  AS null_rows,
    count(DISTINCT id)                  AS distinct_values
FROM public.customer

SELECT
    min(id) AS min_value,
    max(id) AS max_value,
    avg(id::double precision) AS avg_value
FROM public.customer

-- column_stats.sql
-- Per-column null and cardinality profile. Read-only.
-- The column placeholder is a quoted identifier; the relation placeholder is a
-- FROM-clause expression (optionally a LIMIT sample subquery).
SELECT
    count(*)                                  AS total_rows,
    count(*) FILTER (WHERE org_id IS NULL)  AS null_rows,
    count(DISTINCT org_id)                  AS distinct_values
FROM public.customer

SELECT
    min(org_id) AS min_value,
    max(org_id) AS max_value,
    avg(org_id::double precision) AS avg_value
FROM public.customer

-- column_stats.sql
-- Per-column null and cardinality profile. Read-only.
-- The column placeholder is a quoted identifier; the relation placeholder is a
-- FROM-clause expression (optionally a LIMIT sample subquery).
SELECT
    count(*)                                  AS total_rows,
    count(*) FILTER (WHERE email IS NULL)  AS null_rows,
    count(DISTINCT email)                  AS distinct_values
FROM public.customer

SELECT
    avg(length(email::text)) AS avg_length,
    min(length(email::text)) AS min_length,
    max(length(email::text)) AS max_length
FROM public.customer

SELECT status AS value, count(*) AS frequency
FROM public.customer
GROUP BY status
ORDER BY count(*) DESC
LIMIT 20

-- column_stats.sql
-- Per-column null and cardinality profile. Read-only.
-- The column placeholder is a quoted identifier; the relation placeholder is a
-- FROM-clause expression (optionally a LIMIT sample subquery).
SELECT
    count(*)                                  AS total_rows,
    count(*) FILTER (WHERE created_at IS NULL)  AS null_rows,
    count(DISTINCT created_at)                  AS distinct_values
FROM public.customer

SELECT
    min(created_at) AS min_value,
    max(created_at) AS max_value,
    NULL AS avg_value
FROM public.customer

SELECT
    min(created_at) AS earliest,
    max(created_at) AS latest
FROM public.customer
